autopilot.description: Run OpenSpec Autopilot through the openspec-autopilot skill and plugin tools.
autopilot.template: Use the `openspec-autopilot` skill for explicit `/autopilot`, `/autopilot <free-form prompt>`, `autopilot`, active-context `работай`, ready OpenSpec task ledgers, unfinished active OpenSpec changes in `tasks.md` needing explicit `/autopilot` materialization or active_change_handoff, strict task-type phase control, or safe parallel OpenSpec work with plugin/runtime evidence; do not use Autopilot ritual for casual codebase questions, one obvious small edit, `next-step` discovery, direct `openspec-apply-change`, or non-OpenSpec `orchestrator` work when this command was not explicitly invoked. User-supplied command arguments: `$ARGUMENTS`. Classify `$ARGUMENTS` before any claim-capable action: empty or whitespace arguments may call `autopilot_run_next` with no args; exact OpenSpec change scope becomes `changeId`; exact Autopilot ledger task scope becomes `taskId`; exact explicit `changeId` and `taskId` may both be passed only when task-to-change intersection evidence proves they should intersect. Ambiguous exact scopes, unresolved explicit scope flags, or incompatible `changeId`/`taskId` values require an ambiguity blocker or user-choice options; do not call `autopilot_run_next` until resolved. A free-form prompt is not a scope id: do not pass `/autopilot <free-form prompt>` text as `changeId` or `taskId`, inspect queue state read-only with `autopilot_status`, report existing queue work separately using derived queue summary evidence, and do not use `autopilot_run_next` to advance unrelated queued work. Do not persist or echo raw free-form prompt text in plugin-owned state or automation evidence by default; report derived fields such as prompt family, recommended workflow, queue summary, and resolved scope when one exists. Route bugfix, research, planning, or unclear free-form prompt intake to `openspec-explore`; route stable feature, refactor, tooling, config, performance, or protocol prompt intake to `openspec-propose`; route one obvious docs or typo prompt to direct edit; route to `openspec-apply-change` only after an exact existing scope is selected. Interpret JSON output from Autopilot tools, prefer returned `nextActions[]`, report `reasonCode`, summarize `taskSummaries[]`, explain deterministic `selection`, honor `loopGuard`, use `nextRecommendedCall` only as a compatibility fallback, ask only returned blocker questions, and do not manually edit automation ledgers or simulate plugin-owned state transitions. For `ledger_materialized`, report the created `<ledgerRoot>/<change>/automation/task.json` (default `<ledgerRoot>` is `openspec/changes`), `tasksAdvanced[]` validation evidence, and returned `nextActions[]`; no implementation worker was claimed, and a scoped follow-up `autopilot_run_next` is safe only when returned because the previous no-ledger state changed. For `active_change_handoff`, immediately continue the selected unfinished active OpenSpec change through `openspec-apply-change` using `selection.selectedTaskId` and do not repeat `autopilot_run_next`. For `ready_runtime_deferred`, `no_ledgers`, `no_actionable_tasks`, stale evidence, or evidence conflict, do not repeat equivalent no-progress tool calls; hand off to `next-step`, `openspec-apply-change`, manual direct work, `orchestrator`, or follow-up OpenSpec work as appropriate. `no_ledgers` means neither applicable Autopilot ledgers nor unfinished active OpenSpec changes were found.
